action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 18's SpectralAdapter training started at a val PESQ of 2.1222 (already far below the pretrained baseline of ~2.78) and peaked at 2.2389, never recovering. All adapter and fine-tuning attempts have degraded performance because the training losses (L1, MR-STFT, STFT-magnitude) do not align with PESQ and push the model away from the adversarially-trained optimum. The pretrained MetricGAN+ still holds the best test PESQ of 3.134. Analysis: Instead of training anything else, I should exploit the fact that the pretrained model is already near-SOTA and try inference-time post-processing. A simple but often effective trick is mixing the enhanced output back with the noisy input (residual/noisy mix), which can reduce musical noise/artifacts at the cost of slight noise leakage. The optimal mixing weight alpha can be found by grid search on the validation set. This requires no training, no gradients, and zero risk of catastrophic forgetting. Decision: Write a quick script to grid-search alpha on the full validation set, test values from 0.6 to 1.0. If any alpha improves validation PESQ over 1.0 (pure enhanced), update predict.py to use the best alpha and submit eval.
